articles.headline: India Inaugurates First Private Military Aircraft Manufacturing Facility in Vadodara
articles.explainer:
• The facility, a joint venture between Airbus and Tata Advanced Systems Limited (TASL), was inaugurated in Vadodara, Gujarat.
• It marks a significant milestone in India's 'Make in India' and 'Aatmanirbhar Bharat' initiatives in the defense sector.
• The plant will manufacture the C295 transport aircraft for the Indian Air Force, replacing its aging Avro 748 fleet.
• This venture boosts indigenous defense production capabilities, technology transfer, and job creation, positioning India as a global defense manufacturing hub.
• The project underscores the government's commitment to reducing reliance on foreign defense imports and fostering a robust domestic aerospace ecosystem.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment.
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology.
Security challenges and their management in border areas; linkages of organized crime with terrorism.
Various Security forces and agencies and their mandate.
prelimsPoints: First private military aircraft manufacturing facility in India.
Joint venture between Airbus and Tata Advanced Systems Limited (TASL).
Located in Vadodara, Gujarat.
Will manufacture C295 transport aircraft.
C295 aircraft to replace Indian Air Force's Avro 748 fleet.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of private sector involvement in defense production for national security and economic growth.
Analyze how such ventures contribute to 'Make in India' and 'Aatmanirbhar Bharat' objectives, including technology absorption and job creation.
Examine the challenges and opportunities in establishing India as a global defense manufacturing hub.
Evaluate the role of foreign collaborations in accelerating defense indigenization and technology transfer.
articles.imageDescription: Rendering of the C295 transport aircraft being assembled at the Vadodara facility.
articles.trivia: The C295 is a new-generation tactical airlifter in the light and medium segments, capable of carrying up to nine tonnes of payload or 71 personnel.

articles.headline: Nine Injured in Stampede at Mumbai's Bandra Terminus
articles.explainer:
• A stampede occurred at the Bandra Terminus railway station in Mumbai, injuring nine people, two critically.
• The incident reportedly happened due to overcrowding on a footbridge, exacerbated by a sudden rush of passengers for a local train.
• Emergency services responded swiftly, and the injured were shifted to nearby hospitals for treatment.
• This incident highlights persistent challenges in crowd management, especially during festive seasons or peak travel hours at major transit hubs.
• Calls for improved infrastructure, better crowd control mechanisms, and real-time monitoring at railway stations have resurfaced.
gsSubTopics: Urbanization, their problems and their remedies.
Disaster and disaster management.
prelimsPoints: Location: Bandra Terminus railway station, Mumbai.
Cause: Overcrowding on footbridge and rush for train.
Number of injured: Nine, with two critically injured.
mainsPoints: Analyze the factors contributing to stampedes at public places, particularly in urban transit systems.
Suggest comprehensive measures for effective crowd management and public safety at railway stations and other large gatherings.
Discuss the role of urban planning, infrastructure development, and technology in preventing such incidents.
articles.imageDescription: Ambulances and emergency personnel at Bandra Terminus.
articles.trivia: Bandra Terminus is one of the five major railway terminals in Mumbai, serving as a hub for long-distance trains.

articles.headline: Over 150 Injured in Fireworks Storage Facility Explosion in Kerala
articles.explainer:
• An explosion caused by a fire at a fireworks storage facility near the Veerarkavu temple in Kasargod, Kerala, injured at least 150 people.
• The incident occurred during preparations for a local festival, highlighting risks associated with the storage and handling of pyrotechnics.
• Many of the injured sustained burn injuries and were admitted to various hospitals, with some in critical condition.
• Authorities have launched an investigation into the cause of the fire and are reviewing safety protocols for fireworks manufacturing and storage.
• The event has reignited debates on stricter enforcement of regulations and public awareness campaigns regarding fireworks safety.
gsSubTopics: Disaster and disaster management.
Environmental pollution and degradation.
Social empowerment.
prelimsPoints: Location: Near Veerarkavu temple in Kasargod, Kerala.
Type of incident: Fire and explosion at a fireworks storage facility.
Number of injured: At least 150 people.
Context: Occurred during preparations for a local festival.
mainsPoints: Analyze the socio-economic and regulatory challenges associated with the fireworks industry in India.
Discuss the gaps in existing safety regulations and enforcement mechanisms for hazardous materials storage.
Propose measures for enhancing industrial safety, disaster preparedness, and public awareness to prevent such tragedies.
articles.imageDescription: Emergency services attending to the site of the explosion in Kasargod.
articles.trivia: Kerala has a rich tradition of fireworks displays during temple festivals, often leading to debates on balancing cultural practices with safety concerns.

articles.headline: Supreme Court Clarifies Scope of Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023
articles.explainer:
• The Supreme Court, in a landmark ruling, provided crucial clarifications on the interpretation and application of the Digital Personal Data Protection Act (DPDP Act), 2023.
• The judgment specifically addressed concerns regarding the balance between individual right to privacy and legitimate state interests, including surveillance.
• It emphasized the responsibilities of data fiduciaries in handling personal data, mandating strict compliance with consent mechanisms and data minimization principles.
• The ruling is expected to have significant implications for technology companies, government agencies, and citizens in the digital sphere.
• Legal experts note that the decision will help streamline the implementation of the DPDP Act and strengthen data governance in India.
gsSubTopics: Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure.
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
Cyber security.
prelimsPoints: Digital Personal Data Protection Act (DPDP Act) 2023.
Supreme Court ruling on interpretation and application.
Focus on right to privacy vs. state interests.
Data Fiduciaries' responsibilities highlighted.
Significance for data governance and tech companies.
mainsPoints: Critically examine the implications of the Supreme Court's ruling on the DPDP Act for individual privacy and state surveillance in India.
Discuss the challenges in balancing data protection with national security and economic interests in the digital age.
Analyze the role of the DPDP Act in fostering a secure and trustworthy digital ecosystem in India.
Suggest further reforms needed to strengthen India's data governance framework.
articles.imageDescription: Symbolic image of digital data security and privacy.
articles.trivia: The DPDP Act, 2023, replaced the Personal Data Protection Bill, 2019, after extensive parliamentary deliberation.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: Polity & Governance
prelimsSyllabus.topic: Right to Privacy, Data Protection Law

articles.headline: India Positioned as Key Player in Reshaping Global Manufacturing Supply Chains
articles.explainer:
• Geopolitical shifts and the need for diversification away from traditional manufacturing hubs are positioning India as an attractive alternative.
• Government initiatives like the Production Linked Incentive (PLI) schemes and infrastructure development are boosting domestic manufacturing capabilities.
• India's large domestic market, skilled workforce, and democratic stability are key factors drawing international investment.
• Efforts are underway to integrate India more deeply into global value chains, especially in sectors like electronics, pharmaceuticals, and automotive.
• This strategic positioning aims to enhance India's economic resilience and reduce global supply chain vulnerabilities.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment.
Effects of liberalization on the economy, changes in industrial policy and their effects on industrial growth.
Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
prelimsPoints: India's role in global supply chain diversification.
Key drivers: Geopolitical shifts, PLI schemes, infrastructure.
Sectors of focus: Electronics, pharma, automotive.
Advantages: Large market, skilled workforce, democratic stability.
mainsPoints: Analyze the opportunities and challenges for India in becoming a major hub in global manufacturing supply chains.
Evaluate the effectiveness of government policies like PLI schemes and infrastructure development in achieving this goal.
Discuss the geopolitical implications of supply chain diversification and India's role in a multipolar world.
Suggest measures to further enhance India's competitiveness and attractiveness as a manufacturing destination.
articles.imageDescription: A graphic depicting interconnected global supply chains with India highlighted.
articles.trivia: The PLI scheme was introduced in 2020 to boost domestic manufacturing and attract investments in 14 key sectors.

articles.headline: India Bolsters Strategic Ties with Central Asian Nations at Regional Dialogue
articles.explainer:
• India participated in a high-level regional dialogue, reinforcing its strategic and economic engagement with Central Asian countries.
• Discussions focused on enhancing connectivity through projects like the International North-South Transport Corridor (INSTC) and Chabahar Port.
• Key areas of cooperation included energy security, counter-terrorism efforts, trade, and cultural exchanges.
• India emphasized its 'Connect Central Asia' policy, aiming for greater integration and mutual prosperity with the region.
• The dialogue is crucial for India's extended neighborhood policy and for diversifying its energy and security partnerships.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighborhood- relations.
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora.
prelimsPoints: India's 'Connect Central Asia' policy.
Key projects: INSTC, Chabahar Port.
Areas of cooperation: Energy, counter-terrorism, trade.
Participants: India and Central Asian nations (e.g., Kazakhstan, Uzbekistan, Tajikistan, Turkmenistan, Kyrgyzstan).
mainsPoints: Evaluate the strategic importance of Central Asia for India's foreign policy, especially in terms of energy security and regional stability.
Discuss how connectivity projects like INSTC and Chabahar Port enhance India's economic and strategic outreach to the region.
Analyze the challenges and opportunities in India's engagement with Central Asian countries amidst evolving geopolitical dynamics.
articles.imageDescription: Map showing India and Central Asian countries with connectivity routes.
articles.trivia: The International North-South Transport Corridor (INSTC) is a 7,200-km multi-modal network of ship, rail, and road routes for moving freight between India, Iran, Azerbaijan, Russia, Central Asia, and Europe.

articles.headline: ISRO Launches Advanced Earth Observation Satellite for Enhanced Climate Monitoring
articles.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) successfully launched an advanced Earth Observation Satellite (EOS) from Sriharikota.
• The satellite is equipped with high-resolution remote sensing instruments to provide critical data for climate monitoring, environmental studies, and natural resource management.
• It will enhance India's capabilities in tracking atmospheric changes, ocean dynamics, land degradation, and urban heat islands.
• The data from this mission will aid in better disaster management, agricultural planning, and understanding the impacts of climate change.
• This launch reinforces India's commitment to leveraging space technology for sustainable development and global climate action.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology.
Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
prelimsPoints: Launch by ISRO from Sriharikota.
Type of satellite: Advanced Earth Observation Satellite (EOS).
Purpose: Climate monitoring, environmental studies, natural resource management.
Capabilities: Tracking atmospheric changes, ocean dynamics, land degradation, urban heat islands.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of Earth Observation Satellites in addressing contemporary environmental challenges and supporting sustainable development goals.
Analyze India's contributions through ISRO in advancing space technology for climate action and disaster management.
Examine how data from such missions can be integrated into policy-making for agriculture, urban planning, and environmental conservation.
articles.imageDescription: Artistic rendering of an Earth Observation Satellite orbiting the Earth.
articles.trivia: ISRO operates one of the largest constellations of remote sensing satellites globally, providing vital data for various applications.

articles.headline: National Green Hydrogen Mission Shows Promising Progress in Decarbonization Efforts
articles.explainer:
• The National Green Hydrogen Mission has reported significant progress in its objectives to establish India as a global hub for green hydrogen production and export.
• Recent investments in renewable energy projects and electrolyzer manufacturing facilities are accelerating the mission's pace.
• Green hydrogen is crucial for decarbonizing hard-to-abate sectors like refining, fertilizers, steel, and heavy-duty transport.
• The mission aims to reduce fossil fuel imports, create new jobs, and contribute to India's net-zero targets by 2070.
• Policy support, including incentives for production and demand creation, is driving private sector participation and technological innovation.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
articles.source: Ministry of New and Renewable Energy
prelimsPoints: National Green Hydrogen Mission's objective: Global hub for production and export.
Key sectors for decarbonization: Refining, fertilizers, steel, heavy transport.
Contribution to India's net-zero target (2070).
Mechanism: Electrolyzer manufacturing, renewable energy investments.
mainsPoints: Analyze the role of green hydrogen in India's energy transition and its potential to achieve net-zero emissions.
Discuss the economic and environmental benefits of the National Green Hydrogen Mission, including job creation and reduced import dependence.
Examine the technological and infrastructural challenges in scaling up green hydrogen production and its distribution.
Suggest policy interventions to further accelerate the adoption and commercialization of green hydrogen in India.
articles.imageDescription: Illustration of a green hydrogen production plant with wind turbines and solar panels.
articles.trivia: Green hydrogen is produced by electrolysis of water using renewable energy, making it a zero-emission fuel.

articles.headline: Centre Proposes Comprehensive Prison Reforms to Modernize Correctional Facilities
articles.explainer:
• The Union government has unveiled a comprehensive proposal for prison reforms aimed at modernizing correctional facilities across the country.
• The reforms address critical issues such as overcrowding, welfare of undertrial prisoners, vocational training, and rehabilitation programs.
• Emphasis is placed on humanizing prison conditions, integrating technology for better management, and ensuring adherence to human rights standards.
• The proposal includes provisions for alternative dispute resolution mechanisms and community-based corrections to reduce incarceration rates.
• This initiative seeks to transform prisons from mere detention centers into genuine correctional and rehabilitative institutions.
gsSubTopics: Functions and responsibilities of the Union and the States, issues and challenges pertaining to the federal structure, devolution of powers and finances up to local levels and challenges therein.

Welfare schemes for vulnerable sections of the population by the Centre and States and the performance of these schemes; mechanisms, laws, institutions and Bodies constituted for the protection and betterment of these vulnerable sections.
prelimsPoints: Focus areas: Overcrowding, undertrial welfare, vocational training, rehabilitation.
Key aspects: Humanizing conditions, technology integration, human rights adherence.
Provisions for: Alternative dispute resolution, community-based corrections.
Objective: Transform prisons into correctional institutions.
mainsPoints: Critically analyze the existing challenges faced by India's prison system, including overcrowding and human rights concerns.
Discuss the proposed prison reforms and their potential impact on the criminal justice system and rehabilitation of prisoners.
Examine the role of technology and community-based interventions in modernizing correctional facilities.
Suggest measures to ensure effective implementation of prison reforms, considering the federal structure of India.
articles.imageDescription: Symbolic image of prison bars with a ray of light, representing reforms.
articles.trivia: Prisons are a 'State Subject' under the Seventh Schedule of the Indian Constitution, meaning states have primary responsibility for their administration.

articles.headline: India's Digital Public Infrastructure Expanding, Driving Financial Inclusion and Governance
articles.explainer:
• India's Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI), encompassing platforms like Aadhaar, UPI, DigiLocker, and ONDC, continues its rapid expansion.
• This infrastructure is playing a transformative role in enhancing financial inclusion, enabling seamless digital payments, and improving public service delivery.
• The success of DPI models like UPI has garnered international recognition, with several countries exploring similar frameworks.
• It fosters a vibrant startup ecosystem, promoting innovation in fintech, e-commerce, and various other sectors.
• DPI is crucial for India's digital economy, ensuring equitable access to digital services and transparent governance.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment.
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
prelimsPoints: Examples of Indian DPI: Aadhaar, UPI, DigiLocker, ONDC.
Impact: Financial inclusion, digital payments, public service delivery.
International recognition for UPI model.
Role in startup ecosystem and digital economy.
mainsPoints: Analyze the transformative impact of India's Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI) on financial inclusion, governance, and the overall economy.
Discuss how DPI can be leveraged to achieve sustainable development goals and bridge the digital divide.
Examine the challenges in expanding and securing DPI, including data privacy and cyber security concerns.
Suggest measures to foster international collaboration for the adoption and development of DPI models globally.
articles.imageDescription: Infographic showing interconnected digital platforms like UPI, Aadhaar, and DigiLocker.
articles.trivia: UPI (Unified Payments Interface) recorded over 11 billion transactions in September 2024, cementing its position as a leading real-time payment system globally.

articles.headline: India Reiterates Call for Comprehensive Reforms of UN Security Council
articles.explainer:
• India, at the United Nations General Assembly, once again strongly advocated for comprehensive reforms of the UN Security Council (UNSC).
• It highlighted the anachronistic nature of the current UNSC structure, which does not reflect the geopolitical realities of the 21st century.
• India stressed the need for equitable representation, particularly for developing countries and major contributors to global peace and security.
• The call for reform emphasizes the urgency of making the UNSC more legitimate, effective, and representative to address contemporary global challenges.
• India's consistent demand for a permanent seat reflects its growing global stature and commitment to multilateralism.
gsSubTopics: Important International institutions, agencies and fora, their structure, mandate.
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.
prelimsPoints: India's consistent demand for UNSC reforms.
Reason: Current structure not reflecting 21st-century realities.
Demand for equitable representation, especially for developing countries.
Goal: More legitimate, effective, and representative UNSC.
mainsPoints: Analyze the arguments put forth by India and other developing countries for the reform of the UN Security Council.
Discuss the challenges and roadblocks in achieving comprehensive UNSC reforms, considering the interests of the P5 nations.
Evaluate India's candidacy for a permanent seat in the UNSC, highlighting its contributions to global peace and security.
Examine how a reformed UNSC could better address contemporary global challenges like climate change, pandemics, and terrorism.
articles.imageDescription: Logo of the United Nations with flags of member states.
articles.trivia: The UN Security Council has five permanent members (P5) – China, France, Russia, the United Kingdom, and the United States – and ten non-permanent members elected for two-year terms.

articles.headline: Indian Navy Conducts Large-Scale Coastal Security Exercise 'Sagar Kavach'
articles.explainer:
• The Indian Navy, in coordination with various maritime security agencies, conducted a large-scale coastal security exercise named 'Sagar Kavach'.
• The exercise aimed to assess and enhance the preparedness of coastal security mechanisms against potential threats from the sea.
• It involved simulating infiltration attempts, coordinated responses from naval, Coast Guard, police, customs, and fisheries departments.
• 'Sagar Kavach' is crucial for improving inter-agency coordination, maritime domain awareness, and protecting critical coastal infrastructure.
• The drill reinforces India's robust multi-layered coastal defense strategy following past security breaches.
gsSubTopics: Security challenges and their management in border areas; linkages of organized crime with terrorism.
Various Security forces and agencies and their mandate.
prelimsPoints: Name of exercise: 'Sagar Kavach'.
Conducted by: Indian Navy and other maritime security agencies.
Objective: Enhance coastal security preparedness, inter-agency coordination.
Involved: Navy, Coast Guard, police, customs, fisheries departments.
mainsPoints: Discuss the multi-layered approach to coastal security adopted by India and the significance of exercises like 'Sagar Kavach'.
Analyze the challenges in ensuring comprehensive coastal security, including vast coastline, diverse threats, and coordination among multiple agencies.
Suggest measures to further strengthen maritime domain awareness and inter-agency cooperation for effective coastal defense.
Examine the role of technology and local communities in bolstering coastal security.
articles.imageDescription: Indian Navy ships and Coast Guard vessels participating in a coastal security drill.
articles.trivia: The 26/11 Mumbai terror attacks in 2008 significantly spurred India's efforts to strengthen its coastal security architecture.

articles.headline: Indian Cities Explore Strategies to Mitigate Urban Heat Island Effect
articles.explainer:
• A growing number of Indian cities are exploring and implementing strategies to combat the Urban Heat Island (UHI) effect.
• The UHI effect causes urban areas to be significantly warmer than surrounding rural areas due to human activities and built environments.
• Mitigation strategies include promoting green infrastructure (parks, urban forests), cool roofs, reflective pavements, and climate-responsive urban planning.
• These efforts aim to reduce energy consumption for cooling, improve public health, and enhance urban climate resilience.
• The initiatives are part of broader climate action plans to make cities more sustainable and livable in the face of rising temperatures.
gsSubTopics: Urbanization, their problems and their remedies.
Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.

Important Geophysical phenomena such as earthquakes, Tsunami, Volcanic activity, cyclone etc., geographical features and their location-changes in critical geographical features (including water-bodies and ice-caps) and in flora and fauna and the effects of such changes.
prelimsPoints: Urban Heat Island (UHI) effect: Urban areas warmer than rural.
Causes: Built environment, human activities.
Mitigation strategies: Green infrastructure, cool roofs, reflective pavements, urban planning.
Benefits: Reduced energy use, improved public health, climate resilience.
mainsPoints: Explain the Urban Heat Island effect and its causes, particularly in the context of rapidly urbanizing Indian cities.
Discuss various mitigation strategies being adopted by Indian cities to combat UHI and their effectiveness.
Analyze the challenges in implementing green infrastructure and climate-responsive urban planning in dense urban environments.
Suggest integrated policy approaches to make Indian cities more resilient to climate change and extreme heat events.
articles.imageDescription: Satellite image showing temperature differences between urban and rural areas.
articles.trivia: Cool roofs, which reflect more sunlight and absorb less heat, can reduce indoor temperatures by 2-5°C, significantly cutting air conditioning costs.

articles.headline: Post-Monsoon Assessment: Impact on Rabi Crop Sowing and Agricultural Outlook
articles.explainer:
• A post-monsoon assessment indicates varied impacts on Rabi crop sowing across different regions of India, influenced by rainfall distribution.
• While some areas received adequate late-monsoon showers boosting soil moisture, others faced deficits affecting sowing prospects.
• Government agencies are monitoring reservoir levels and providing advisories to farmers on crop choices and irrigation practices.
• The outlook for agricultural output, particularly for wheat, pulses, and oilseeds, is being carefully evaluated amidst global food price volatility.
• This assessment is crucial for informing agricultural policies, ensuring food security, and managing potential inflation.
gsSubTopics: Major crops cropping patterns in various parts of the country, different types of irrigation and irrigation system storage, transport and marketing of agricultural produce and issues and related constraints; e-technology in the aid of farmers.

Issues related to direct and indirect farm subsidies and minimum support prices; Public Distribution System— objectives, functioning, limitations, revamping; issues of buffer stocks and food security; Technology missions; economics of animal-rearing.
prelimsPoints: Rabi crops: Sown in winter, harvested in spring (e.g., wheat, barley, gram, peas, mustard).
Impact of late monsoon on Rabi sowing.
Monitoring of reservoir levels.
Role of government advisories for farmers.
mainsPoints: Analyze the critical role of monsoon rainfall in India's agricultural economy and its implications for Rabi crop production and food security.
Discuss the challenges faced by Indian agriculture due to climate variability and the need for climate-resilient farming practices.
Evaluate the effectiveness of government interventions, such as irrigation schemes and farmer advisories, in mitigating agricultural risks.
Suggest policy measures to ensure stable agricultural output and manage food inflation in the long term.
articles.imageDescription: Farmers working in a field during Rabi crop sowing season.
articles.trivia: Rabi is derived from the Arabic word for 'spring', referring to the season when these crops are harvested.

articles.headline: Discussions Emerge on Strengthening Centre-State Fiscal Relations Amidst Economic Shifts
articles.explainer:
• Discussions have emerged regarding the need to re-evaluate and strengthen Centre-State fiscal relations in India, driven by evolving economic realities.
• Key issues include the distribution of tax revenues, GST compensation mechanisms, and the allocation of funds for centrally sponsored schemes.
• The role of the Finance Commission in ensuring equitable distribution and fiscal federalism remains central to these debates.
• States advocate for greater fiscal autonomy and a larger share in the divisible pool of taxes to meet their developmental needs.
• Strengthening fiscal federalism is crucial for balanced regional development and effective implementation of national policies.
gsSubTopics: Functions and responsibilities of the Union and the States, issues and challenges pertaining to the federal structure, devolution of powers and finances up to local levels and challenges therein.
Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment.
prelimsPoints: Key issues: Tax revenue distribution, GST compensation, centrally sponsored schemes.
Role of Finance Commission (Article 280).
States' demand: Greater fiscal autonomy, larger share in divisible pool.
Importance: Balanced regional development, effective policy implementation.
mainsPoints: Critically analyze the current state of Centre-State fiscal relations in India and the challenges faced by states in managing their finances.
Discuss the role and recommendations of the Finance Commission in fostering fiscal federalism and ensuring equitable resource distribution.
Examine the impact of GST and centrally sponsored schemes on the fiscal autonomy of states.
Suggest reforms to strengthen Centre-State fiscal cooperation and promote balanced economic development across regions.
articles.imageDescription: Graphic illustrating the flow of funds between central and state governments.
articles.trivia: The Goods and Services Tax (GST) subsumed various indirect taxes, necessitating a new mechanism for compensation to states for revenue losses.

articles.headline: Progress Report on National Education Policy (NEP) Implementation Across States
articles.explainer:
• A comprehensive progress report on the implementation of the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020 across various states has been released.
• The report highlights successes in foundational literacy and numeracy programs, vocational education integration, and digital learning initiatives.
• Challenges remain in teacher training, infrastructure development, and ensuring equitable access to quality education in remote areas.
• States are adopting varied approaches to align their educational structures with NEP guidelines, reflecting the policy's flexible framework.
• The NEP aims to transform India's education system to meet the demands of the 21st century, focusing on holistic, multidisciplinary learning.
gsSubTopics: Development processes and the development industry — the role of NGOs, SHGs, various groups and associations, donors, charities, institutional and other stakeholders.
Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources.
prelimsPoints: NEP 2020 implementation progress report.
Successes: Foundational literacy, vocational education, digital learning.
Challenges: Teacher training, infrastructure, equitable access.
Objective: Holistic, multidisciplinary learning for 21st century skills.
mainsPoints: Analyze the key features and objectives of the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020 and its potential to transform India's education system.
Discuss the progress made and the challenges encountered in the implementation of NEP across different states.
Evaluate the role of vocational education and digital learning in making education more relevant and accessible.
Suggest measures to overcome implementation hurdles and ensure equitable access to quality education under NEP.
articles.imageDescription: Students engaging in modern classroom activities, representing NEP objectives.
articles.trivia: NEP 2020 is the third major education policy in India's history, after those of 1968 and 1986.

articles.headline: India and Pakistan Hold Technical Discussions on Indus Waters Treaty
articles.explainer:
• India and Pakistan held technical discussions under the framework of the Indus Waters Treaty (IWT) in a bid to resolve ongoing disputes.
• The talks focused on specific technical aspects of hydroelectric projects being developed by India on the Western rivers.
• Both sides reiterated their commitment to upholding the provisions of the treaty while addressing concerns through dialogue.
• The IWT, brokered by the World Bank in 1960, governs the sharing of waters of the Indus River system.
• Sustaining dialogue on water-sharing is crucial for regional stability and cooperation between the two nuclear-armed neighbors.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighborhood- relations.
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora.
prelimsPoints: Indus Waters Treaty (IWT) signed in 1960.
Mediated by: World Bank.
Governs water sharing of Indus River system between India and Pakistan.
Focus of talks: Technical aspects of Indian hydroelectric projects on Western rivers.
Western Rivers: Indus, Jhelum, Chenab (Pakistan's exclusive use, India can use for non-consumptive purposes).
mainsPoints: Analyze the significance of the Indus Waters Treaty (IWT) as a model for transboundary water sharing and its role in India-Pakistan relations.
Discuss the persistent challenges and disputes arising from the IWT, particularly concerning hydroelectric projects.
Examine the implications of water scarcity and climate change on the future of water sharing between riparian states in the region.
Suggest pathways for enhancing water diplomacy and cooperation between India and Pakistan beyond the IWT framework.
articles.imageDescription: Map of the Indus River system showing the distribution of rivers between India and Pakistan.
articles.trivia: Despite numerous conflicts, the Indus Waters Treaty is one of the most successful water-sharing agreements globally, having survived wars and political tensions between India and Pakistan.

articles.headline: Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission Expands Reach, Focus on Health Records Digitization
articles.explainer:
• The Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission (ABDM) has reported significant expansion in its reach, focusing on the digitization of health records across India.
• The mission aims to create a seamless online platform for healthcare, enabling easy access to digital health IDs, doctor consultations, and medical records.
• This expansion is crucial for improving healthcare accessibility, efficiency, and transparency, especially in remote and underserved areas.
• Key initiatives include increasing registrations for ABHA (Ayushman Bharat Health Account) and integrating more healthcare providers into the digital ecosystem.
• ABDM is a foundational step towards universal health coverage and a more resilient public health system in India.
gsSubTopics: Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources.
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
prelimsPoints: Mission: Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission (ABDM).
Objective: Digitization of health records, seamless online healthcare platform.
Key component: ABHA (Ayushman Bharat Health Account).
Benefits: Improved accessibility, efficiency, transparency in healthcare.
Nodal agency: National Health Authority (NHA).
mainsPoints: Discuss the transformative potential of the Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission (ABDM) in revolutionizing healthcare delivery in India.
Analyze the challenges and opportunities associated with the digitization of health records and creating a unified digital health ecosystem.
Examine how ABDM can contribute to achieving universal health coverage and addressing health disparities across different regions.
Suggest measures to ensure data privacy, security, and digital literacy for the successful and equitable implementation of ABDM.
articles.imageDescription: Graphic showing a digital health ID card and interconnected healthcare services.
articles.trivia: ABDM is built on the principles of openness, interoperability, and security, aiming to create a robust national digital health ecosystem.

articles.headline: Government Initiatives Boost Startup Ecosystem in Tier-2 and Tier-3 Cities
articles.explainer:
• Government initiatives and funding programs are increasingly focusing on fostering startup ecosystems in India's Tier-2 and Tier-3 cities.
• This decentralization aims to unlock entrepreneurial potential beyond major metropolitan areas, promoting inclusive economic growth.
• Support includes incubation centers, mentorship programs, easier access to capital, and policy incentives for local startups.
• The rise of remote work and improved digital infrastructure are further enabling this shift, attracting talent and investment.
• Developing these regional hubs is crucial for job creation, innovation, and balanced industrial development across the country.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment.
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
Inclusive growth and issues arising from it.
prelimsPoints: Focus on startup ecosystem in Tier-2 and Tier-3 cities.
Initiatives: Incubation centers, mentorship, capital access, policy incentives.
Drivers: Remote work, digital infrastructure.
Benefits: Job creation, innovation, balanced development.
mainsPoints: Analyze the significance of developing startup ecosystems in Tier-2 and Tier-3 cities for India's inclusive economic growth and innovation landscape.
Discuss the various government initiatives and policy interventions aimed at supporting entrepreneurship in these regions.
Examine the challenges faced by startups in smaller cities compared to metropolitan areas and suggest measures to overcome them.
Evaluate the role of digital infrastructure and remote work trends in enabling the decentralization of the startup ecosystem.
articles.imageDescription: Modern office space with young entrepreneurs in a Tier-2 city.
articles.trivia: India is home to the third-largest startup ecosystem globally, with a growing number of unicorns emerging from non-metro cities.

highlights: India inaugurates its first private military aircraft manufacturing facility in Vadodara, a major boost for 'Make in India' defense.
Supreme Court clarifies the scope of the Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023, enhancing data governance and privacy frameworks.
ISRO successfully launches an advanced Earth Observation Satellite for enhanced climate monitoring and environmental studies.
Over 150 people injured in a fireworks storage facility explosion in Kerala, raising concerns about safety regulations during festive preparations.
India strengthens strategic and economic ties with Central Asian nations, emphasizing connectivity projects and counter-terrorism cooperation.

prelimsMocks.question:
With reference to the recent inauguration of India's first private military aircraft manufacturing facility, consider the following statements:
1. The facility is a joint venture between Airbus and Hindustan Aeronautics Limited (HAL).
2. It is located in Bengaluru, Karnataka.
3. It will manufacture the C295 transport aircraft for the Indian Air Force.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is incorrect: The facility is a joint venture between Airbus and Tata Advanced Systems Limited (TASL), not HAL. Statement 2 is incorrect: The facility is located in Vadodara, Gujarat, not Bengaluru. Statement 3 is correct: The plant will manufacture the C295 transport aircraft for the Indian Air Force.

prelimsMocks.question: Which of the following statements best describes the primary objective of the Digital Personal Data Protection Act (DPDP Act), 2023?
options: (a) To regulate social media platforms and prevent misinformation.

(b) To provide for the processing of digital personal data in a manner that recognizes both the right of individuals to protect their personal data and the need to process such personal data for lawful purposes.
(c) To establish a national cybersecurity agency to combat cyber terrorism.
(d) To mandate government access to all digital data for national security purposes.
prelimsMocks.answer: (b) To provide for the processing of digital personal data in a manner that recognizes both the right of individuals to protect their personal data and the need to process such personal data for lawful purposes.
prelimsMocks.explanation: The DPDP Act, 2023, is India's first comprehensive law on data privacy, aiming to balance individual data protection rights with legitimate needs for data processing, as clarified by the Supreme Court. Options (a), (c), and (d) describe other related but not primary objectives of this specific Act.

prelimsMocks.question:
Consider the following about India's National Green Hydrogen Mission:
1. Its primary aim is to establish India as a global hub for green hydrogen production and export.
2. Green hydrogen is produced by electrolysis of water using fossil fuels.
3. The mission targets decarbonization of sectors like refining, fertilizers, and steel.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is correct: The mission aims to make India a global hub for green hydrogen. Statement 2 is incorrect: Green hydrogen is produced using renewable energy for electrolysis, not fossil fuels. Statement 3 is correct: Decarbonization of hard-to-abate sectors like refining, fertilizers, steel, and heavy-duty transport is a key objective.

prelimsMocks.question: The 'Sagar Kavach' exercise, recently conducted by the Indian Navy, is related to:
options: (a) Anti-piracy operations in the Indian Ocean.
(d) Joint naval drills with friendly foreign countries.
prelimsMocks.explanation: The 'Sagar Kavach' is a large-scale coastal security exercise involving the Indian Navy and various maritime security agencies to enhance preparedness and coordination against sea-borne threats.

prelimsMocks.question: With respect to Centre-State fiscal relations in India, the Finance Commission is a constitutional body established under which Article of the Indian Constitution?
prelimsMocks.explanation: The Finance Commission is constituted by the President of India under Article 280 of the Constitution, primarily to define the financial relations between the Union government and the individual state governments.

mainsMocks.question: Discuss the strategic significance of private sector participation in defense manufacturing for India's 'Aatmanirbhar Bharat' vision. What challenges remain in achieving full indigenization in this critical sector?
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS3: Security challenges and their management in border areas; linkages of organized crime with terrorism. Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment. Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology.
mainsMocks.approach: Start by defining 'Aatmanirbhar Bharat' in defense. Explain the strategic rationale for private sector entry (reducing import dependence, fostering innovation, job creation, exports). Discuss the benefits like technology transfer, efficiency, and scale. Then, delve into challenges such as bureaucratic hurdles, funding gaps for R&D, limited testing infrastructure, intellectual property rights issues, and competition with established PSUs. Conclude with recommendations for policy reforms.

mainsMocks.question: Critically examine the implications of the Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023, on individual privacy and state surveillance in India. Suggest measures to strike a balance between data protection and national security.
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS2: Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure. Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation. GS3: Cyber security.
mainsMocks.approach: Introduce the DPDP Act and its objective. Analyze its provisions related to individual rights (consent, right to erasure, etc.) and data fiduciary obligations, highlighting how it strengthens privacy. Then, discuss the provisions that allow exemptions for state agencies (national security, public order) and the concerns regarding potential for surveillance. Critically evaluate the balance struck by the Act. Finally, suggest measures like robust oversight mechanisms, independent judicial review of surveillance requests, clear definitions of 'national security', and public transparency to ensure a fair balance.

mainsMocks.question: Evaluate India's growing engagement with Central Asian countries. How do connectivity projects like INSTC and Chabahar Port contribute to India's strategic and economic interests in the region?
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS2: India and its neighborhood- relations. Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests. Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora.
mainsMocks.approach: Begin by outlining India's 'Connect Central Asia' policy and its historical/cultural linkages. Explain the strategic imperatives (energy security, counter-terrorism, regional stability, counter-balancing other powers). Detail how INSTC provides a multi-modal trade route bypassing Pakistan, reducing transit time and cost. Explain how Chabahar Port serves as a crucial gateway to Afghanistan and Central Asia, enhancing India's access to resource-rich regions. Conclude by discussing the challenges (geopolitical competition, regional instability) and the potential for deeper collaboration.

quickBytes.text: The C295 transport aircraft, to be manufactured in Vadodara, will replace the Indian Air Force's aging Avro 748 fleet.
The Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023, is India's first comprehensive law on data privacy.
National Green Hydrogen Mission aims to make India a global hub for green hydrogen production and export by 2030.
The Finance Commission is a constitutional body established under Article 280 to define financial relations between the Centre and states.
Chabahar Port, located in Iran, is a key component of India's vision for connectivity to Afghanistan and Central Asia, bypassing Pakistan.
ISRO's PSLV (Polar Satellite Launch Vehicle) is known as the 'workhorse' launch vehicle for its reliability in placing satellites.
The 'Make in India' initiative, launched in 2014, aims to encourage companies to manufacture in India and boost local production.
The Urban Heat Island (UHI) effect refers to urban areas being significantly warmer than surrounding rural areas.
NEP 2020 (National Education Policy) is the third major education policy in India, after those of 1968 and 1986.
ABHA (Ayushman Bharat Health Account) is a key component of the Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission (ABDM) for digital health IDs.

GS1.headline: Nine Injured in Stampede at Mumbai's Bandra Terminus
GS1.explainer:
• A stampede occurred at the Bandra Terminus railway station in Mumbai, injuring nine people, two critically.
• The incident reportedly happened due to overcrowding on a footbridge, exacerbated by a sudden rush of passengers for a local train.
• Emergency services responded swiftly, and the injured were shifted to nearby hospitals for treatment.
• This incident highlights persistent challenges in crowd management, especially during festive seasons or peak travel hours at major transit hubs.
• Calls for improved infrastructure, better crowd control mechanisms, and real-time monitoring at railway stations have resurfaced.
gsSubTopics: Urbanization, their problems and their remedies.
Disaster and disaster management.
prelimsPoints: Location: Bandra Terminus railway station, Mumbai.
Cause: Overcrowding on footbridge and rush for train.
Number of injured: Nine, with two critically injured.
mainsPoints: Analyze the factors contributing to stampedes at public places, particularly in urban transit systems.
Suggest comprehensive measures for effective crowd management and public safety at railway stations and other large gatherings.
Discuss the role of urban planning, infrastructure development, and technology in preventing such incidents.
GS1.imageDescription: Ambulances and emergency personnel at Bandra Terminus.
GS1.trivia: Bandra Terminus is one of the five major railway terminals in Mumbai, serving as a hub for long-distance trains.

GS1.headline: Indian Cities Explore Strategies to Mitigate Urban Heat Island Effect
GS1.explainer:
• A growing number of Indian cities are exploring and implementing strategies to combat the Urban Heat Island (UHI) effect.
• The UHI effect causes urban areas to be significantly warmer than surrounding rural areas due to human activities and built environments.
• Mitigation strategies include promoting green infrastructure (parks, urban forests), cool roofs, reflective pavements, and climate-responsive urban planning.
• These efforts aim to reduce energy consumption for cooling, improve public health, and enhance urban climate resilience.
• The initiatives are part of broader climate action plans to make cities more sustainable and livable in the face of rising temperatures.
gsSubTopics: Urbanization, their problems and their remedies.
Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.

Important Geophysical phenomena such as earthquakes, Tsunami, Volcanic activity, cyclone etc., geographical features and their location-changes in critical geographical features (including water-bodies and ice-caps) and in flora and fauna and the effects of such changes.
prelimsPoints: Urban Heat Island (UHI) effect: Urban areas warmer than rural.
Causes: Built environment, human activities.
Mitigation strategies: Green infrastructure, cool roofs, reflective pavements, urban planning.
Benefits: Reduced energy use, improved public health, climate resilience.
mainsPoints: Explain the Urban Heat Island effect and its causes, particularly in the context of rapidly urbanizing Indian cities.
Discuss various mitigation strategies being adopted by Indian cities to combat UHI and their effectiveness.
Analyze the challenges in implementing green infrastructure and climate-responsive urban planning in dense urban environments.
Suggest integrated policy approaches to make Indian cities more resilient to climate change and extreme heat events.
GS1.imageDescription: Satellite image showing temperature differences between urban and rural areas.
GS1.trivia: Cool roofs, which reflect more sunlight and absorb less heat, can reduce indoor temperatures by 2-5°C, significantly cutting air conditioning costs.

GS2.headline: Supreme Court Clarifies Scope of Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023
GS2.explainer:
• The Supreme Court, in a landmark ruling, provided crucial clarifications on the interpretation and application of the Digital Personal Data Protection Act (DPDP Act), 2023.
• The judgment specifically addressed concerns regarding the balance between individual right to privacy and legitimate state interests, including surveillance.
• It emphasized the responsibilities of data fiduciaries in handling personal data, mandating strict compliance with consent mechanisms and data minimization principles.
• The ruling is expected to have significant implications for technology companies, government agencies, and citizens in the digital sphere.
• Legal experts note that the decision will help streamline the implementation of the DPDP Act and strengthen data governance in India.
gsSubTopics: Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure.
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
Cyber security.
prelimsPoints: Digital Personal Data Protection Act (DPDP Act) 2023.
Supreme Court ruling on interpretation and application.
Focus on right to privacy vs. state interests.
Data Fiduciaries' responsibilities highlighted.
Significance for data governance and tech companies.
mainsPoints: Critically examine the implications of the Supreme Court's ruling on the DPDP Act for individual privacy and state surveillance in India.
Discuss the challenges in balancing data protection with national security and economic interests in the digital age.
Analyze the role of the DPDP Act in fostering a secure and trustworthy digital ecosystem in India.
Suggest further reforms needed to strengthen India's data governance framework.
GS2.imageDescription: Symbolic image of digital data security and privacy.
GS2.trivia: The DPDP Act, 2023, replaced the Personal Data Protection Bill, 2019, after extensive parliamentary deliberation.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: Polity & Governance
prelimsSyllabus.topic: Right to Privacy, Data Protection Law

GS2.headline: India Bolsters Strategic Ties with Central Asian Nations at Regional Dialogue
GS2.explainer:
• India participated in a high-level regional dialogue, reinforcing its strategic and economic engagement with Central Asian countries.
• Discussions focused on enhancing connectivity through projects like the International North-South Transport Corridor (INSTC) and Chabahar Port.
• Key areas of cooperation included energy security, counter-terrorism efforts, trade, and cultural exchanges.
• India emphasized its 'Connect Central Asia' policy, aiming for greater integration and mutual prosperity with the region.
• The dialogue is crucial for India's extended neighborhood policy and for diversifying its energy and security partnerships.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighborhood- relations.
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora.
prelimsPoints: India's 'Connect Central Asia' policy.
Key projects: INSTC, Chabahar Port.
Areas of cooperation: Energy, counter-terrorism, trade.
Participants: India and Central Asian nations (e.g., Kazakhstan, Uzbekistan, Tajikistan, Turkmenistan, Kyrgyzstan).
mainsPoints: Evaluate the strategic importance of Central Asia for India's foreign policy, especially in terms of energy security and regional stability.
Discuss how connectivity projects like INSTC and Chabahar Port enhance India's economic and strategic outreach to the region.
Analyze the challenges and opportunities in India's engagement with Central Asian countries amidst evolving geopolitical dynamics.
GS2.imageDescription: Map showing India and Central Asian countries with connectivity routes.
GS2.trivia: The International North-South Transport Corridor (INSTC) is a 7,200-km multi-modal network of ship, rail, and road routes for moving freight between India, Iran, Azerbaijan, Russia, Central Asia, and Europe.

GS2.headline: Centre Proposes Comprehensive Prison Reforms to Modernize Correctional Facilities
GS2.explainer:
• The Union government has unveiled a comprehensive proposal for prison reforms aimed at modernizing correctional facilities across the country.
• The reforms address critical issues such as overcrowding, welfare of undertrial prisoners, vocational training, and rehabilitation programs.
• Emphasis is placed on humanizing prison conditions, integrating technology for better management, and ensuring adherence to human rights standards.
• The proposal includes provisions for alternative dispute resolution mechanisms and community-based corrections to reduce incarceration rates.
• This initiative seeks to transform prisons from mere detention centers into genuine correctional and rehabilitative institutions.
gsSubTopics: Functions and responsibilities of the Union and the States, issues and challenges pertaining to the federal structure, devolution of powers and finances up to local levels and challenges therein.

Welfare schemes for vulnerable sections of the population by the Centre and States and the performance of these schemes; mechanisms, laws, institutions and Bodies constituted for the protection and betterment of these vulnerable sections.
prelimsPoints: Focus areas: Overcrowding, undertrial welfare, vocational training, rehabilitation.
Key aspects: Humanizing conditions, technology integration, human rights adherence.
Provisions for: Alternative dispute resolution, community-based corrections.
Objective: Transform prisons into correctional institutions.
mainsPoints: Critically analyze the existing challenges faced by India's prison system, including overcrowding and human rights concerns.
Discuss the proposed prison reforms and their potential impact on the criminal justice system and rehabilitation of prisoners.
Examine the role of technology and community-based interventions in modernizing correctional facilities.
Suggest measures to ensure effective implementation of prison reforms, considering the federal structure of India.
GS2.imageDescription: Symbolic image of prison bars with a ray of light, representing reforms.
GS2.trivia: Prisons are a 'State Subject' under the Seventh Schedule of the Indian Constitution, meaning states have primary responsibility for their administration.

GS2.headline: India Reiterates Call for Comprehensive Reforms of UN Security Council
GS2.explainer:
• India, at the United Nations General Assembly, once again strongly advocated for comprehensive reforms of the UN Security Council (UNSC).
• It highlighted the anachronistic nature of the current UNSC structure, which does not reflect the geopolitical realities of the 21st century.
• India stressed the need for equitable representation, particularly for developing countries and major contributors to global peace and security.
• The call for reform emphasizes the urgency of making the UNSC more legitimate, effective, and representative to address contemporary global challenges.
• India's consistent demand for a permanent seat reflects its growing global stature and commitment to multilateralism.
gsSubTopics: Important International institutions, agencies and fora, their structure, mandate.
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.
prelimsPoints: India's consistent demand for UNSC reforms.
Reason: Current structure not reflecting 21st-century realities.
Demand for equitable representation, especially for developing countries.
Goal: More legitimate, effective, and representative UNSC.
mainsPoints: Analyze the arguments put forth by India and other developing countries for the reform of the UN Security Council.
Discuss the challenges and roadblocks in achieving comprehensive UNSC reforms, considering the interests of the P5 nations.
Evaluate India's candidacy for a permanent seat in the UNSC, highlighting its contributions to global peace and security.
Examine how a reformed UNSC could better address contemporary global challenges like climate change, pandemics, and terrorism.
GS2.imageDescription: Logo of the United Nations with flags of member states.
GS2.trivia: The UN Security Council has five permanent members (P5) – China, France, Russia, the United Kingdom, and the United States – and ten non-permanent members elected for two-year terms.

GS2.headline: Discussions Emerge on Strengthening Centre-State Fiscal Relations Amidst Economic Shifts
GS2.explainer:
• Discussions have emerged regarding the need to re-evaluate and strengthen Centre-State fiscal relations in India, driven by evolving economic realities.
• Key issues include the distribution of tax revenues, GST compensation mechanisms, and the allocation of funds for centrally sponsored schemes.
• The role of the Finance Commission in ensuring equitable distribution and fiscal federalism remains central to these debates.
• States advocate for greater fiscal autonomy and a larger share in the divisible pool of taxes to meet their developmental needs.
• Strengthening fiscal federalism is crucial for balanced regional development and effective implementation of national policies.
gsSubTopics: Functions and responsibilities of the Union and the States, issues and challenges pertaining to the federal structure, devolution of powers and finances up to local levels and challenges therein.
Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment.
prelimsPoints: Key issues: Tax revenue distribution, GST compensation, centrally sponsored schemes.
Role of Finance Commission (Article 280).
States' demand: Greater fiscal autonomy, larger share in divisible pool.
Importance: Balanced regional development, effective policy implementation.
mainsPoints: Critically analyze the current state of Centre-State fiscal relations in India and the challenges faced by states in managing their finances.
Discuss the role and recommendations of the Finance Commission in fostering fiscal federalism and ensuring equitable resource distribution.
Examine the impact of GST and centrally sponsored schemes on the fiscal autonomy of states.
Suggest reforms to strengthen Centre-State fiscal cooperation and promote balanced economic development across regions.
GS2.imageDescription: Graphic illustrating the flow of funds between central and state governments.
GS2.trivia: The Goods and Services Tax (GST) subsumed various indirect taxes, necessitating a new mechanism for compensation to states for revenue losses.

GS2.headline: Progress Report on National Education Policy (NEP) Implementation Across States
GS2.explainer:
• A comprehensive progress report on the implementation of the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020 across various states has been released.
• The report highlights successes in foundational literacy and numeracy programs, vocational education integration, and digital learning initiatives.
• Challenges remain in teacher training, infrastructure development, and ensuring equitable access to quality education in remote areas.
• States are adopting varied approaches to align their educational structures with NEP guidelines, reflecting the policy's flexible framework.
• The NEP aims to transform India's education system to meet the demands of the 21st century, focusing on holistic, multidisciplinary learning.
gsSubTopics: Development processes and the development industry — the role of NGOs, SHGs, various groups and associations, donors, charities, institutional and other stakeholders.
Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources.
prelimsPoints: NEP 2020 implementation progress report.
Successes: Foundational literacy, vocational education, digital learning.
Challenges: Teacher training, infrastructure, equitable access.
Objective: Holistic, multidisciplinary learning for 21st century skills.
mainsPoints: Analyze the key features and objectives of the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020 and its potential to transform India's education system.
Discuss the progress made and the challenges encountered in the implementation of NEP across different states.
Evaluate the role of vocational education and digital learning in making education more relevant and accessible.
Suggest measures to overcome implementation hurdles and ensure equitable access to quality education under NEP.
GS2.imageDescription: Students engaging in modern classroom activities, representing NEP objectives.
GS2.trivia: NEP 2020 is the third major education policy in India's history, after those of 1968 and 1986.

GS2.headline: India and Pakistan Hold Technical Discussions on Indus Waters Treaty
GS2.explainer:
• India and Pakistan held technical discussions under the framework of the Indus Waters Treaty (IWT) in a bid to resolve ongoing disputes.
• The talks focused on specific technical aspects of hydroelectric projects being developed by India on the Western rivers.
• Both sides reiterated their commitment to upholding the provisions of the treaty while addressing concerns through dialogue.
• The IWT, brokered by the World Bank in 1960, governs the sharing of waters of the Indus River system.
• Sustaining dialogue on water-sharing is crucial for regional stability and cooperation between the two nuclear-armed neighbors.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighborhood- relations.
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora.
prelimsPoints: Indus Waters Treaty (IWT) signed in 1960.
Mediated by: World Bank.
Governs water sharing of Indus River system between India and Pakistan.
Focus of talks: Technical aspects of Indian hydroelectric projects on Western rivers.
Western Rivers: Indus, Jhelum, Chenab (Pakistan's exclusive use, India can use for non-consumptive purposes).
mainsPoints: Analyze the significance of the Indus Waters Treaty (IWT) as a model for transboundary water sharing and its role in India-Pakistan relations.
Discuss the persistent challenges and disputes arising from the IWT, particularly concerning hydroelectric projects.
Examine the implications of water scarcity and climate change on the future of water sharing between riparian states in the region.
Suggest pathways for enhancing water diplomacy and cooperation between India and Pakistan beyond the IWT framework.
GS2.imageDescription: Map of the Indus River system showing the distribution of rivers between India and Pakistan.
GS2.trivia: Despite numerous conflicts, the Indus Waters Treaty is one of the most successful water-sharing agreements globally, having survived wars and political tensions between India and Pakistan.

GS2.headline: Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission Expands Reach, Focus on Health Records Digitization
GS2.explainer:
• The Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission (ABDM) has reported significant expansion in its reach, focusing on the digitization of health records across India.
• The mission aims to create a seamless online platform for healthcare, enabling easy access to digital health IDs, doctor consultations, and medical records.
• This expansion is crucial for improving healthcare accessibility, efficiency, and transparency, especially in remote and underserved areas.
• Key initiatives include increasing registrations for ABHA (Ayushman Bharat Health Account) and integrating more healthcare providers into the digital ecosystem.
• ABDM is a foundational step towards universal health coverage and a more resilient public health system in India.
gsSubTopics: Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources.
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
prelimsPoints: Mission: Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission (ABDM).
Objective: Digitization of health records, seamless online healthcare platform.
Key component: ABHA (Ayushman Bharat Health Account).
Benefits: Improved accessibility, efficiency, transparency in healthcare.
Nodal agency: National Health Authority (NHA).
mainsPoints: Discuss the transformative potential of the Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission (ABDM) in revolutionizing healthcare delivery in India.
Analyze the challenges and opportunities associated with the digitization of health records and creating a unified digital health ecosystem.
Examine how ABDM can contribute to achieving universal health coverage and addressing health disparities across different regions.
Suggest measures to ensure data privacy, security, and digital literacy for the successful and equitable implementation of ABDM.
GS2.imageDescription: Graphic showing a digital health ID card and interconnected healthcare services.
GS2.trivia: ABDM is built on the principles of openness, interoperability, and security, aiming to create a robust national digital health ecosystem.

GS3.headline: India Inaugurates First Private Military Aircraft Manufacturing Facility in Vadodara
GS3.explainer:
• The facility, a joint venture between Airbus and Tata Advanced Systems Limited (TASL), was inaugurated in Vadodara, Gujarat.
• It marks a significant milestone in India's 'Make in India' and 'Aatmanirbhar Bharat' initiatives in the defense sector.
• The plant will manufacture the C295 transport aircraft for the Indian Air Force, replacing its aging Avro 748 fleet.
• This venture boosts indigenous defense production capabilities, technology transfer, and job creation, positioning India as a global defense manufacturing hub.
• The project underscores the government's commitment to reducing reliance on foreign defense imports and fostering a robust domestic aerospace ecosystem.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment.
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology.
Security challenges and their management in border areas; linkages of organized crime with terrorism.
Various Security forces and agencies and their mandate.
prelimsPoints: First private military aircraft manufacturing facility in India.
Joint venture between Airbus and Tata Advanced Systems Limited (TASL).
Located in Vadodara, Gujarat.
Will manufacture C295 transport aircraft.
C295 aircraft to replace Indian Air Force's Avro 748 fleet.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of private sector involvement in defense production for national security and economic growth.
Analyze how such ventures contribute to 'Make in India' and 'Aatmanirbhar Bharat' objectives, including technology absorption and job creation.
Examine the challenges and opportunities in establishing India as a global defense manufacturing hub.
Evaluate the role of foreign collaborations in accelerating defense indigenization and technology transfer.
GS3.imageDescription: Rendering of the C295 transport aircraft being assembled at the Vadodara facility.
GS3.trivia: The C295 is a new-generation tactical airlifter in the light and medium segments, capable of carrying up to nine tonnes of payload or 71 personnel.

GS3.headline: Over 150 Injured in Fireworks Storage Facility Explosion in Kerala
GS3.explainer:
• An explosion caused by a fire at a fireworks storage facility near the Veerarkavu temple in Kasargod, Kerala, injured at least 150 people.
• The incident occurred during preparations for a local festival, highlighting risks associated with the storage and handling of pyrotechnics.
• Many of the injured sustained burn injuries and were admitted to various hospitals, with some in critical condition.
• Authorities have launched an investigation into the cause of the fire and are reviewing safety protocols for fireworks manufacturing and storage.
• The event has reignited debates on stricter enforcement of regulations and public awareness campaigns regarding fireworks safety.
gsSubTopics: Disaster and disaster management.
Environmental pollution and degradation.
Social empowerment.
prelimsPoints: Location: Near Veerarkavu temple in Kasargod, Kerala.
Type of incident: Fire and explosion at a fireworks storage facility.
Number of injured: At least 150 people.
Context: Occurred during preparations for a local festival.
mainsPoints: Analyze the socio-economic and regulatory challenges associated with the fireworks industry in India.
Discuss the gaps in existing safety regulations and enforcement mechanisms for hazardous materials storage.
Propose measures for enhancing industrial safety, disaster preparedness, and public awareness to prevent such tragedies.
GS3.imageDescription: Emergency services attending to the site of the explosion in Kasargod.
GS3.trivia: Kerala has a rich tradition of fireworks displays during temple festivals, often leading to debates on balancing cultural practices with safety concerns.

GS3.headline: India Positioned as Key Player in Reshaping Global Manufacturing Supply Chains
GS3.explainer:
• Geopolitical shifts and the need for diversification away from traditional manufacturing hubs are positioning India as an attractive alternative.
• Government initiatives like the Production Linked Incentive (PLI) schemes and infrastructure development are boosting domestic manufacturing capabilities.
• India's large domestic market, skilled workforce, and democratic stability are key factors drawing international investment.
• Efforts are underway to integrate India more deeply into global value chains, especially in sectors like electronics, pharmaceuticals, and automotive.
• This strategic positioning aims to enhance India's economic resilience and reduce global supply chain vulnerabilities.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment.
Effects of liberalization on the economy, changes in industrial policy and their effects on industrial growth.
Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
prelimsPoints: India's role in global supply chain diversification.
Key drivers: Geopolitical shifts, PLI schemes, infrastructure.
Sectors of focus: Electronics, pharma, automotive.
Advantages: Large market, skilled workforce, democratic stability.
mainsPoints: Analyze the opportunities and challenges for India in becoming a major hub in global manufacturing supply chains.
Evaluate the effectiveness of government policies like PLI schemes and infrastructure development in achieving this goal.
Discuss the geopolitical implications of supply chain diversification and India's role in a multipolar world.
Suggest measures to further enhance India's competitiveness and attractiveness as a manufacturing destination.
GS3.imageDescription: A graphic depicting interconnected global supply chains with India highlighted.
GS3.trivia: The PLI scheme was introduced in 2020 to boost domestic manufacturing and attract investments in 14 key sectors.

GS3.headline: ISRO Launches Advanced Earth Observation Satellite for Enhanced Climate Monitoring
GS3.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) successfully launched an advanced Earth Observation Satellite (EOS) from Sriharikota.
• The satellite is equipped with high-resolution remote sensing instruments to provide critical data for climate monitoring, environmental studies, and natural resource management.
• It will enhance India's capabilities in tracking atmospheric changes, ocean dynamics, land degradation, and urban heat islands.
• The data from this mission will aid in better disaster management, agricultural planning, and understanding the impacts of climate change.
• This launch reinforces India's commitment to leveraging space technology for sustainable development and global climate action.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology.
Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
prelimsPoints: Launch by ISRO from Sriharikota.
Type of satellite: Advanced Earth Observation Satellite (EOS).
Purpose: Climate monitoring, environmental studies, natural resource management.
Capabilities: Tracking atmospheric changes, ocean dynamics, land degradation, urban heat islands.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of Earth Observation Satellites in addressing contemporary environmental challenges and supporting sustainable development goals.
Analyze India's contributions through ISRO in advancing space technology for climate action and disaster management.
Examine how data from such missions can be integrated into policy-making for agriculture, urban planning, and environmental conservation.
GS3.imageDescription: Artistic rendering of an Earth Observation Satellite orbiting the Earth.
GS3.trivia: ISRO operates one of the largest constellations of remote sensing satellites globally, providing vital data for various applications.

GS3.headline: National Green Hydrogen Mission Shows Promising Progress in Decarbonization Efforts
GS3.explainer:
• The National Green Hydrogen Mission has reported significant progress in its objectives to establish India as a global hub for green hydrogen production and export.
• Recent investments in renewable energy projects and electrolyzer manufacturing facilities are accelerating the mission's pace.
• Green hydrogen is crucial for decarbonizing hard-to-abate sectors like refining, fertilizers, steel, and heavy-duty transport.
• The mission aims to reduce fossil fuel imports, create new jobs, and contribute to India's net-zero targets by 2070.
• Policy support, including incentives for production and demand creation, is driving private sector participation and technological innovation.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
GS3.source: Ministry of New and Renewable Energy
prelimsPoints: National Green Hydrogen Mission's objective: Global hub for production and export.
Key sectors for decarbonization: Refining, fertilizers, steel, heavy transport.
Contribution to India's net-zero target (2070).
Mechanism: Electrolyzer manufacturing, renewable energy investments.
